item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I was really bummed about about the image being removed, so I am in your debt for taking the time to do that. I no longer have the job where I had access to Photoshop and a proper PC, so it was very much a relief.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Ah, that's a relief. While the previous link was disabled, I vaguely remembered the original was a depressed man holding his head in his hands. I thought maybe I can suggest some similer images…

So I googled "Depressed Wikimedia", and surprisingly he was there :D

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yes, that is the original! Thank you so much!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I checked it out and, actually, there may be no problem at all. Original image is this guy, right? It's under CC BY-SA 2.0!

Source: Wikimedia Commons
License: CC BY-SA 2.0
Title: Depressed (4649749639).jpg
Author: Sander van der Wel
Release year: 2013
Note: Heavily modified by trennerdios for this article.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The image is such a heavy edit of the original photo that there is virtually nothing left of the original, and it is absolutely absurd to remove it as it is pretty much original artwork at this point. I was originally contacted about my images in regard to the image policy, explained each of them, and then never received any further contact about them. To go through and remove the images from nearly all my articles without further contact or warning is insulting.

item.title: Operational Staff Post
item.content:encoded: Original website did not release this image under an explicit Creative Commons license and it is assumed not compliant with CC 3.0. As such, it has since been removed.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I liked it, then I didn't, then I liked it again. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I thought I had upvoted this when I first read it, but apparently not, since it just let me upvote it "again". This is the kind of thing that's hard to make work, but you made it work :).

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Just realized this broke 100. I'm glad it was this one, as it's probably my favorite, or perhaps 2nd favorite, of the few I've written.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm not entirely sure how I was trying to be meta. Regardless, I don't know what you would need to get this, since you weren't specific at all about what doesn't make sense.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It seems to me that every time you read an article that is the slightest bit confusing, you don't try to give it any thought in the hopes of reaching a deeper understanding before immediately downvoting. Even if you can't understand it, you can go to the discussion page to try to get a hint from others, then fill yourself in with any information gleaned from them. Just saying that everything is too cryptic isn't going to help anybody.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: What? Doesn't make sense. I get that you are trying to be meta, but its too cryptic. -1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: That…that is pretty much 100% spot on.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This was delightfully unsettling.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: My headcanon is that it actually is a reality bender who is clinically depressed, possibly BPD, and is caught between just wanting to make people happy and just wanting to disappear and knows it has these terrible mood swings but it CAN'T HELP IT it's trying as hard as it can and really the swings are all that are left and even they are broken and JUST STAY AWAY and let it disappear and then it can't harm anything if it's not there but please don't think it's bad it's just broken.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I…I can't believe I never thought of that myself!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I can't believe you didn't name it Mood Swing.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
*looks at picture*
…Pokemon Black? Is that you?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Eurgh… that picture is really creeping me out. Alongside that, the rest of the article is really good. I would agree with an earlier comment that maybe there's too much external commentary especially towards the beginning of the log. Still enthusiastically upvoting though!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: any article where the format screw isnt immediately obvious gets an instant +1 to me, and stays that way if backed up by solid writing and a solid idea. this does all of that

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Okay, now I kind of feel bad…

Which is good, as far as prose is concerned.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: My thoughts exactly. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is great and I forbid you from not making any more. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I… What…?

+1, in part because because that last footnote is lovely

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I almost did the same thing. This is a great read.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It's a clinically depressed swing set. Beyond that, it's a very broken soul in the form of a swing set. It's so broken that it distorts reality unintentionally.

I'm not sure that it's a particularly interesting idea at its core, and I don't expect that to sway you from a neutral vote, but just thought I'd clarify.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: To be painfully honest, I'm not really sold on this idea. What I just read looked like an emotional swing set, and that's it. But it is decently written, so no vote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: That's an intentional error, and it's not the only one in the description.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Four swings but only five of them work, hmm.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I think I like this.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
and fitted with four plastic swings, five of which are broken

I almost posted to say this should be fixed. Good thing I read further. I like the hell outta this, and that's not a very easy thing to make happen.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I actually think this is one of the better format screws on the site. I really, really fuckin dig it, so of course I'm upvoting it.
I never thought I'd have feelings about a damn swingset.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
While the idea can work, personally I feel it's too 'in your face'.
I won't deny, I was interested/confused when the description stated there were more groken swings than the total swings… but after that it's just more andmore of the same.

Maybe if it were a bit more subtle on the first logs, I would reconsiderate. No vote from me for now.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I totally get what you're saying. I trimmed it a little bit, but there's a lot that I wanted to include, so it may not be enough to change your mind. I wouldn't mind taking out some more of those lines, but I'll have to scrutinize them some more to decide if what's there is truly necessary. Appreciate the feedback.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I like the idea of the SCP altering the written document about it, but towards the end it seemed like it was the researcher writing it that was the one who was having an emotional crisis. That angle is often attempted and often unsuccessful.

I also think the effect would be more powerful if there wasn't one out of place statement in every paragraph. It creates too a predictable rhythm. A statement like "It isn't trying to do this. It doesn't want to hurt anything." should come out of nowhere and be very jarring when surrounded by a clinical tone.

No vote for now.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Final entry in my playground equipment series, if this survives. Tone issues and most errors are intentional, FYI.

Thank you to everyone in chat who gave me critique and advice. It was much needed, and appreciated.

Original image was located by C-Dives: http://www.scp-wiki.net/forum/t-1001103/scp-2483#post-4641384

It was heavily edited by me.
